pattern: Divisibility by 25 depends only on a number's last two digits, so maximizing kept digits reduces to finding the latest achievable two-digit ending as a subsequence.
hints: A number is divisible by 25 exactly when it ends in 00, 25, 50, or 75, or is the single digit 0.
To keep as many digits as possible, you want to keep every digit before some position i, then digit i, then one later digit j, where the pair (digit i, digit j) matches one of the four valid endings.

For each of the four endings, scan from the right to find the largest i whose digit matches the ending's first character and that has the ending's second character appearing somewhere after it; the best such i across all four endings gives the longest keepable prefix. If none exists, keep a single '0' digit if one exists, otherwise delete everything.

edge_inputs.description: no zero anywhere and no achievable ending, must delete everything
only a lone zero is achievable, no two-digit ending exists
a 00 ending is achievable only using non-adjacent digits

stress.description: random digit strings without leading zeros across the length range
